title: Grammitis basiattenuata
taxonomy: Grammitis basiattenuata belongs to the family Polypodiaceae, which is a family of ferns. This species is classified as a type of epiphytic fern, meaning it grows on other plants. It is part of the genus Grammitis, which contains a range of species of small to medium-sized ferns. Grammitis basiattenuata is native to Central and South America and is often found in tropical regions. The exact classification of Grammitis basiattenuata may vary depending on the source, but it is commonly classified as a species of Grammitid ferns.

This plant is rich in health benefits, particularly in its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which have been shown to aid in wound healing, reduce oxidative stress, and combat various diseases.
